The Weekly Digest

Issue #47 · January 19, 2024

This Week's Top Stories

1. Rust Foundation Announces New Grant Program

The Rust Foundation has launched a $2M grant program to support critical infrastructure projects in the Rust ecosystem. Applications open February 1st.

Read more →

2. SQLite 4.0 Proposal Surfaces

A proposal for SQLite 4.0 with native JSON columns and improved concurrent write support has generated significant discussion in the database community.

Read more →

3. WebAssembly GC Ships in All Major Browsers

With Firefox's latest release, all major browsers now support the WebAssembly Garbage Collection proposal, enabling languages like Kotlin and Dart to compile to smaller Wasm binaries.

Read more →

Tool of the Week: Helix Editor

Helix is a post-modern terminal text editor written in Rust with Kakoune-inspired editing and built-in LSP support. It's a compelling alternative to Neovim for those who want sensible defaults out of the box.

You're receiving this because you subscribed at newsletter.example.com
